findings.analysis: Significant thermal variations indicate possible insulation deficiencies. Hot spots on south-facing slope correlate with missing shingles and inadequate insulation coverage.
anomalies: Localized high temperatures (42°C) at roof edges suggesting air leakage
Temperature differential of 7°C across roof surface indicates uneven insulation distribution
findings.severity: HIGH - Indicates energy loss and potential moisture infiltration pathways

findings.analysis: Thermal imaging shows cold spots (18°C) at areas with mortar deterioration visible in inspection. Suggests moisture infiltration and possibly compromised wall insulation.
anomalies: Cold band following mortar line pattern
Temperature drops 10°C at corners where caulking is cracked
Indicates active thermal bridging through compromised seals
findings.severity: HIGH - Suggests active water infiltration and thermal bridging

findings.analysis: Furnace shows elevated surface temperature (35°C during afternoon). Ducts in attic show inconsistent temperatures indicating poor insulation and inefficient distribution.
anomalies: Ductwork temperature variation suggests insulation gaps
Hot spots on furnace casing indicate potential efficiency issues
Temperature loss along uninsulated duct runs
findings.severity: MEDIUM - Indicates efficiency loss but not immediate safety concern

findings.analysis: Water heater surface temperature elevated (52°C) suggesting heavy usage or insulation loss. Base area shows slight temperature variation consistent with reported water stains.
anomalies: Temperature anomaly near base (50°C vs surroundings 22°C)
Surface temperature suggests inadequate tank insulation
findings.severity: MEDIUM - Indicates age-related efficiency loss and possible minor leakage

findings.analysis: Panel temperature normal (24°C). No hotspots detected indicating no immediate electrical issues. However, older aluminum wiring visible elsewhere shows expected thermal signature.
anomalies: No critical anomalies detected at panel
findings.severity: LOW - Thermal imaging shows no current electrical safety concerns

findings.analysis: Foundation shows temperature pattern consistent with concrete thermal properties. Cold spots (15°C) in corners align with visible hairline cracks, suggesting possible air infiltration.
anomalies: Cold air infiltration pattern visible at crack locations
Corner temperatures 5°C lower than wall average
Indicates potential moisture movement through cracks
findings.severity: MEDIUM - Suggests moisture and air infiltration through cracks

overall_analysis: Thermal imaging reveals multiple areas of concern correlating with physical inspection findings. Primary issues: (1) Roof/attic thermal loss and air leakage, (2) Wall envelope compromised by failed seals and mortar deterioration, (3) System efficiency losses in HVAC and water heating. These issues represent significant energy waste and potential moisture control problems.
energy_loss_estimate: Estimated annual heating/cooling cost increase of 15-20% due to identified thermal deficiencies
moisture_risk_assessment: HIGH - Multiple pathways for moisture infiltration identified through thermal imaging
recommended_thermal_improvements: Increase attic insulation to R-38 minimum
Seal all air leakage points at roof penetrations
Repair exterior wall caulking and mortar
Replace or upgrade furnace for efficiency gains
Consider water heater replacement with high-efficiency model

images.description: Thermal image showing temperature gradient across roof - hot spots at south slope
Thermal pattern showing mortar line deterioration with cold air infiltration
Thermal view of uninsulated HVAC ducts showing heat loss
